matches.reasoning: Door of Hope is a growth-stage Christian nonprofit focused on family homelessness — a genuine mission adjacency for Anthony given his community development and behavioral health work. The CDO role calls for fundraising strategy leadership, which is partially in his lane (institutional funding, grant strategy), but the core emphasis appears to be major gifts, individual donor cultivation, and building a development team — areas where his evidence is thin. Salary is at his floor ($150K–$170K), and Pasadena, CA is a location friction point. He's competitive but not a slam-dunk screener.
key_matches: $8.9M blended capital architecture for 52-bed recovery housing facility directly maps to housing and social services mission
Nonprofit Founder and Executive Director at G1VE Atlanta demonstrates C-suite nonprofit leadership and board management
$500K competitive FTA federal grant secured shows direct institutional fundraising success
United Way VIP Board Leadership Development and GA Giving Leadership Council show philanthropy network in the social sector
$50M+ in funding initiatives reflects broad multi-source fundraising strategy knowledge
gaps: Major gifts / individual donor portfolio management is the likely core of this CDO role — not well-evidenced in his background
Pasadena, CA location is a geographic friction point for an Atlanta-based candidate even if hybrid
Christian faith-based organizational culture may require explicit values alignment in screening
No direct evidence of building or managing a development department team from the CDO seat
matches.executive_summary: Door of Hope is a Pasadena-based Christian nonprofit serving families experiencing homelessness, and this CDO role leads the full fundraising strategy including major gifts, corporate partnerships, events, and grants. Anthony's community development background, nonprofit founder/ED experience, and grant strategy track record (including $500K federal grant and $8M blended housing finance) provide real proof points for the housing and social services mission. However, the core of this role appears to be individual major gifts cultivation and donor portfolio management — his weakest lane — which will reduce his competitiveness against career development directors.
matches.resume_angles: ["Lead with $8.9M blended capital for recovery housing \u2014 directly analogous to their housing mission and shows capital strategy depth", "Frame G1VE Atlanta ED role (transit + community development, $500K federal grant) as proof of nonprofit C-suite fundraising leadership", "Highlight $50M+ in supported funding initiatives to demonstrate scope of philanthropic strategy, not just execution"]
matches.risks: ["Screeners will look for a career development professional with a track record of closing major gifts \u2014 he doesn't have this directly", "Faith-based mission fit may require cultural alignment screening beyond professional qualifications", "Salary floor ($150K) is at the bottom of the band \u2014 limited upside"]
matches.outreach_strategy: Identify Door of Hope's Executive Director or Board Chair on LinkedIn and send a warm note connecting the $8.9M recovery housing capital work to their mission before submitting. Frame it as 'I've done the capital and strategy work for organizations like yours' to differentiate from traditional development directors.
matches.title: Chief Development Officer (CDO)
matches.description: Do you want to make a difference in the lives of families facing homelessness? Do you thrive in roles where strategy, relationships, and execution come together to drive lasting impact? Are you searching for not just a job, but a calling? Door of Hope, a Christian nonprofit that serves families facing homelessness, is looking for a Chief Development Officer (CDO) to lead our fundraising strategy and grow the resources that make our mission possible. In this role, you will lead Door of Hope’s fu…
